bullets.text: The World Health Organization (WHO) has warned that the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo is facing a "catastrophic collision" of disease and conflict, hindering efforts to contain its spread.
Israel has issued an evacuation order for parts of southern Lebanon, declaring areas south of the Zahrani River as "combat zones" and threatening Hezbollah with further strikes.
Mexico's President Sheinbaum has allowed the Iranian team to stay in Mexico during the World Cup, after the US declined to host them despite Iran playing group games in the United States.

Legal action has been initiated against the federal environment minister's decision to approve the clearing of nearly 3,000 hectares of threatened species habitat, an area ten times the size of Sydney's CBD.
New modelling predicts that changes to NDIS eligibility rules will result in 241,000 participants being cut from the scheme within four years, meaning they will no longer receive support by mid-2031.
A new report suggests that Australia's armed forces currently lack sufficient strike power to adequately defend the nation.
Oil prices have fallen following reports from Iranian state television detailing a peace proposal that would restore shipping in the Strait of Hormuz within a month.
Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook stated that she is prepared to raise interest rates if inflation persists, noting that the risks remain tilted towards higher inflation.

JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon indicated the bank could spend up to $20 billion on new acquisitions, citing that the Trump administration's lighter regulatory approach has freed up to $50 billion in excess capital.
